advantages: Ships with Python — no install, no config.
Drops you straight into the running state — inspect anything, no rerun.
REPL inside the paused program — you can assign, call, and import freely.
Post-mortem mode investigates exceptions where they happened.
Conditional breakpoints make hot-loop debugging tractable.
disadvantages: CLI-only; no source-panel by default (ipdb/pdb++ help here).
Doesn't work well for code already deep in production (use logging or remote debuggers).
Pausing breaks any timeouts / heartbeats your code has.
Multi-threaded / async programs are clumsy in plain pdb — IDE debuggers handle these better.
Accidentally shipping `breakpoint()` to production is a real risk; CI lint should catch it.
